transfer.how_sota_wins: Three compounding mechanisms, now measured directly from the winners' task-72 artifacts in /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/. (1) THE OPPONENT IS WEAK AND KNOWN: the 'reference' is April-2025 Gemini-2.5-pro Deep Research output, not human, and it is the weakest-insight report in the field. RACE is a single-prompt pairwise contrast (target always article_1), so beating 0.50 means out-writing one specific, year-old LLM house style in a side-by-side read. (2) STRUCTURE THAT MATCHES THE RUBRIC LITERALLY, ADAPTED TO GENRE: cellcog (SOTA family under our GPT-5.5 judge) writes task 72 as 11 H2 / 32 H3 with 178 single-idea paragraphs averaging 69 words — and ZERO bullets/tables on this task (it averages 48 table rows and 45 bullets corpus-wide; it suppresses them because task 72 is a literature review). The readability rubric names 'distinct heading levels' and 'single idea per paragraph' as criteria; tavily's 152-bullet fragment style is the field's worst readability (49.21). Because the judge writes a comparative analysis before scoring EVERY criterion, structural parity also stops the contrast-bleed that currently drags our insight and comprehensiveness. (3) A REPEATABLE FABRICATION-FREE SYNTHESIS PATTERN: cellcog's insight paragraphs are triads — grounded finding A (named study + number) + grounded finding B (conflicting named study) + a hedged reconciliation sentence carrying no new facts ('Both studies use rigorous methods... the divergence is therefore most plausibly attributable to institutional context rather than methodology'), then enumerated candidate explanations and an explicit 'current evidence cannot distinguish among these.' It institutionalizes this in a dedicated 'Critical Synthesis' section (What the Evidence Converges On / Outstanding Tensions / Limitations / Future Research). This is exactly the insight rubric's six criteria (causal mechanisms, second-order effects, challenging conventional wisdom, forward-looking) executed without a single unsourced factual claim. (4) COVERAGE VIA LENGTH DELIVERED IN MANY WRITING STEPS: cellcog's cleaned prose is ~11,200 words vs our 7,692 and the reference's ~7,700; qianfan (58.03 legacy) writes 47k words in 656 short paragraphs; WebWeaver's measured shape is ~25 discrete ~1k-token writing steps off a 4.9k-token outline. Sustained length with one-idea paragraphs is what moves comprehensiveness above parity. Caveat: onyx scores 54.54 (legacy) with only 4,554 words, so length is sufficient, not necessary — density of synthesis moves per section is the common factor.

transferable.technique: Genre-adaptive structural retrofit: 8-9 numbered H2 sections split into 25-32 H3 subsections; 60-150-word single-idea paragraphs with topic sentences; for task 72 specifically, academic-review prose register with FEW OR NO bullets (cellcog uses zero on this task; tavily's bullet-heavy style is punished). Keep the Wave2 comparison matrix — tables ARE rubric-named ('appropriate use of charts and tables') and qianfan/deepinsight use them — but as 1-3 purposeful tables, not decoration.
transferable.evidence: Measured from /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/cellcog.jsonl id=72 (32 H3, 178 paras @69w, 0 bullets/tables) vs tavily.jsonl id=72 (152 bullets, 15w paras, readability 49.21 = its worst dim); CONFIRMED readability rubric criteria in criteria_prompt_en.py; CONFIRMED ArticleCleaner preserves all structure.
transferable.maps_to_our_gap: Readability 0.3774 (worst-anomaly dimension, ~14 pts below the SOTA band) plus contrast-bleed into insight/comprehensiveness via the pairwise judge.
transferable.expected_points: Readability 0.377 -> 0.47-0.50 is +0.013-0.017 overall; contrast-bleed relief on other dims plausibly another +0.005-0.010.
transferable.risk: Low. Structure is judge-visible (CONFIRMED) and orthogonal to grounding. Only risk is bullet overuse — the evidence says restrain it for this genre.

transferable.technique: Synthesis triads + a dedicated 'Critical Synthesis' section: for each contested question, write grounded-finding-A + grounded-finding-B + a hedged reconciliation sentence that introduces NO new numbers/entities ('the divergence is most plausibly attributable to...'), then enumerate 2-3 candidate explanations and state explicitly what the evidence cannot yet distinguish. Add H2 with subsections: convergence / outstanding tensions / limitations of the literature / future research directions.
transferable.evidence: Measured verbatim from cellcog.jsonl id=72 sections 7.1 and 8.2 (Humlum-vs-Brynjolfsson reconciliation; three-tension enumeration); CONFIRMED insight rubric in criteria_prompt_en.py rewards exactly these sentence types; tavily's outline (section 7.1/7.2 consensus-vs-uncertainties) shows the same move; cellcog-max insight = 57.08, the field's best under our judge.
transferable.maps_to_our_gap: Insight 0.4238 at weight 0.32 — our worst dimension at the heaviest weight.
transferable.expected_points: Insight 0.424 -> 0.48-0.52 is +0.018-0.031 overall. This refines our Wave1 'interpretive weave': the deterministic bar on numbers/entities in inference sentences is validated — SOTA's reconciliation sentences carry no new facts — but the ADJACENT grounded sentences must name the studies being reconciled, so pair the weave with named-study attribution in running prose (our Wave2 'naming journals' item, upgraded to naming authors+years).
transferable.risk: None to faithfulness: the pattern is fabrication-free by construction. Risk is quality — a bad reconciliation sentence reads as filler; needs a rubric-scored revision pass.

transferable.technique: Multi-step section composition to expand cleaned-prose coverage from 7.7k to ~11k words: write each H3 in its own LLM call (~20-30 calls, WebWeaver's measured 24.78 writing steps) off a refined global outline, while keeping the full evidence corpus (not just section-scoped spans) available to the writer so cross-section reconciliation sentences remain possible.
transferable.evidence: CONFIRMED WebWeaver Table 3 measurements (25 writing steps, 26k output tokens, 4.9k-token outline, 2.2 outline optimization rounds); measured cellcog 11.2k cleaned prose words and qianfan 47k/59-H3/656-para shape; CONFIRMED warning from arXiv 2601.20843 + Tavily convergent design that section-siloed context kills cross-source synthesis — so sectioned WRITING, global CONTEXT.
transferable.maps_to_our_gap: Comprehensiveness 0.4549 (weight 0.29) and the structural impossibility of 24+ subsections in one monolithic pass.
transferable.expected_points: Comprehensiveness 0.455 -> 0.50 is +0.013; also carries instruction-following (+0.005-0.01) because literature-review conventions (scope/methodology section, future-directions section) come free with the sectioned outline.
transferable.risk: Cost/latency (20-30 calls; mind the <272K codex input cliff) and cross-section redundancy; mitigated by writing from a shared outline with per-section one-line summaries of what other sections already said.

transferable.technique: Rubric-targeted self-revision pass (TTD-DR-style, minimal version): after composition, run one LLM-judge critique against the six insight criteria + three readability criteria, revise once on the textual critique. Not the full evolutionary loop — one critique-revise cycle on the finished draft.
transferable.evidence: CONFIRMED TTD-DR self-evolution mechanism (fitness score + textual critique -> revise -> merge, arXiv 2507.16075); it is the only published mechanism for optimizing prose against a rubric, and our judge prompts are public (criteria_prompt_en.py).
transferable.maps_to_our_gap: Insight and readability jointly — converts 'average band' (4-6) sentences to 'good band' (6-8), which per the confirmed aggregation math is all parity requires.
transferable.expected_points: +0.005-0.015 overall, additive with the above; low-confidence because TTD-DR has no RACE score.
transferable.risk: Revision can dilute grounded claims; run the span-grounding verifier AFTER revision as a gate so the moat is enforced, not assumed.

not_transferable: The actual SOTA systems' internals: cellcog-max, qianfan, WhaleCloud, bodhi, lunon, dalpha are all closed with no papers — everything above is reverse-engineered from their output artifacts. We cannot copy their retrieval depth, backbone model, or any hidden revision machinery, and the last few points of their scores may live exactly there.

TTD-DR's core move — writing the initial draft from parametric knowledge and 'denoising' with retrieval — is a direct violation of span-grounded faithfulness (the draft IS ungrounded generation). Only its critique-revise loop is safe to take; the noisy-draft paradigm is not.

ADORE's memory-locked synthesis: it is a STRICTER section-scoped evidence constraint than we already have, would make our insight problem worse, and its headline benefit (traceability) is invisible to RACE anyway. Wrong system to imitate; confirmed.

qianfan's 47k-word brute-force length: 6x the reference, measured only under the legacy Gemini judge (never rescored under GPT-5.5), and would blow our token budget (codex input cost cliff at 272K). Length beyond ~11-12k cleaned words has no confirmed payoff under our judge.

Exploiting citation-invisibility as a strategy (dumping citation effort entirely): it is a RACE-v1-only quirk; DeepResearch Bench II reintroduces retrieval/InfoRecall scoring, and our faithfulness moat is the product story outside the benchmark. Keep citations; just know the judge cannot see them.

Tavily's architecture wholesale: its headline is token-cost engineering (66% reduction), it ranks 23rd, its bullet-heavy output style is measurably punished, and the widely-circulated 'Reflection Agent / Section Formatter' pipeline attributed to it is tutorial contamination, not Tavily.
transfer.verdict_on_our_gap: The 0.088 gap is doubly miscalibrated and the plan targets a phantom. (1) The reference is not human — it is April-2025 Gemini DR output, the weakest-insight report in the field, so 0.50 is a softer bar than we believed. (2) ADORE 0.5265 is not SOTA on any current board; under our pinned GPT-5.5 judge the real ladder is: parity 0.5000 at gap 0.0618, real SOTA cellcog-max 0.5578 at gap 0.1196. We currently sit between perplexity (0.4305) and openai-DR (0.4784). What the confirmed evidence explains: structural retrofit (+0.018-0.027), synthesis triads + critical-synthesis section (+0.018-0.031), multi-step composition for coverage (+0.018-0.023), one revision pass (+0.005-0.015) — realistic stacked total +0.05-0.08, landing 0.49-0.52. So the published record credibly explains getting to reference parity and slightly past it, i.e. roughly WhaleCloud/bodhi's neighborhood minus a couple of points. It does NOT explain the last ~0.04 to 0.5578: cellcog-max has no paper, its above-parity insight (0.5708, i.e. the judge scoring it ~1.33x the reference on the heaviest dimension) is only partially accounted for by the triad pattern I measured in its artifacts, and every published ablation with numbers (WebWeaver, TTD-DR) is either legacy-judge or non-RACE. Honest bottom line: our Wave1/Wave2 plan is directionally correct but under-specified in three ways the evidence fixes — restrain bullets for this genre (prose register, not brief register), add the named-study reconciliation triads and a dedicated tensions/convergence section rather than diffuse 'hedged inference sentences', and add the coverage/length lever (7.7k -> ~11k cleaned words via ~25 sectioned writing calls with global context) which the current plan omits entirely. Projected ceiling with all confirmed levers: ~0.51-0.52. Beyond that we would be reverse-engineering closed systems, and we should say so rather than promise 0.5578.

summary: Deep multi-modal research into how ADORE (0.5265), Tavily (0.5244) and every >0.50 system beat the human reference on DeepResearch Bench RACE — and what is transferable to POLARIS

phases.title: Sweep
phases.detail: parallel multi-angle search: leaderboard, papers, code, judge-mechanics

phases.title: Deep read
phases.detail: fetch and read the primary sources properly

phases.title: Verify
phases.detail: adversarially check every extracted technique claim

phases.title: Transfer
phases.detail: map verified techniques onto POLARIS gaps; what we can steal, what we cannot
